The surgeon mobilizes the dorsal aspect of the ascending mesocolon and the mesenteric root, preserving the lateral colonic attachments. The dissection progresses from the distal mesoilium in a medial to lateral direction.

The surgeon uses cyanine green enhanced fluorescence to assess bowel perfusion in the distal ileum and transverse colon, determining the appropriate transection line based on the visible perfusion patterns.

Following specimen resection, the surgeon performs a primary anastomosis using a stapler to create a side-to-side isoperistaltic connection between the distal ileum and transverse colon, entirely within the abdominal cavity.
